name: From Valldoreix to Bistró Les Escales – circular through the Collserola Natural Park
name_original: Desde Valldoreix a Bistró Les Escales – circular por el Parque Natural de Collserola
region_intro: Road cycling around Sant Cugat Del Vallès is characterized by its proximity to the expansive Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola, offering a diverse landscape of green, mountainous terrain. The region features numerous hills and varied elevation changes, making it suitable for different levels of road cyclists. Sant Cugat Del Vallès itself is known for its green spaces and a network of streams, providing a pleasant backdrop for outdoor activities. Best road cycling routes around Sant Cugat Del Vallès The most popular road cycling route is From Valldoreix to Bistró Les Escales – circular through the Collserola Natural Park, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Rierada – Santa Creu d'Olorda loop from Volpelleres, a moderate 21.6 miles (34.7 km) path. This loop provides a scenic ride through the local landscape with notable elevation gains. Local road cyclists also love the Creu Aragall Restaurant – Martorell's Devil's Bridge loop from Sant Cugat Centre, a 38.2 miles (61.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 1 minute. Road cycling around Sant Cugat Del Vallès is defined by the mountainous terrain of the Collserola Natural Park, extensive green spaces, and diverse elevation profiles. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate climbs to longer, more challenging rides. The routes in Sant Cugat Del Vallès are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Cugat Del Vallès's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Sant Cugat Del Vallès offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 30 routes available on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect while road cycling in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Road cycling in Sant Cugat Del Vallès is characterized by its proximity to the expansive &lt;strong&gt;Parc Natural de la Serra de Collserola&lt;/strong&gt;, offering a diverse landscape of green, mountainous terrain. You'll encounter numerous hills, varied elevation changes, and picturesque views, including magnificent vistas of Barcelona from certain roads. The area is also known for its 'green city' environment with urban parks and a network of streams.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Yes, there are several options for beginners. Approximately 8 easy road cycling routes are available in Sant Cugat Del Vallès, providing a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes without overly demanding climbs.

Q: What about more advanced or challenging road cycling routes?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Sant Cugat Del Vallès offers routes with significant elevation gains and longer distances. There are 3 difficult routes available, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20624360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Coastal Road – Gelida Road loop from Hospital General&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 120 km with more than 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular way to explore the region. Many routes, especially within the Collserola Natural Park, are designed as loops. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31364304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rierada – Santa Creu d'Olorda loop from Volpelleres&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 34.7 km ride with notable elevation gains.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Sant Cugat Del Vallès benefits from a temperate Mediterranean climate, making road cycling enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, while summer can be warm, and winter is typically mild.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. While cycling, you can explore points of interest within the Collserola Natural Park such as the ancient Pi d'en Xandri pine tree or the Sant Medir Shrine. The region also features historical sites like the Monastery of Sant Cugat. For those interested in historical structures, you might find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2500852" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Baró Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/951471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellciuró Ruins&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Is it possible to access road cycling routes using public transport?
A: Yes, Sant Cugat Del Vallès is well-connected. Many routes are designed to connect to Rodalies-Renfe or FGC train stations, making it convenient for cyclists traveling with their bikes to access the starting points of various routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: While many road cycling routes involve significant elevation, some of the easier routes can be suitable for families with older children or those looking for a less strenuous ride. The 'green city' environment and urban green spaces also offer pleasant, flatter areas for casual cycling.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: The road cycling routes around Sant Cugat Del Vallès are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 5,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the challenge offered by the Collserola Natural Park.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Sant Cugat Del Vallès?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer rides, options extend beyond the immediate vicinity. A notable long-distance route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15253081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gavà - Begues Climb – Church of Olesa de Bonesvalls loop from Hospital General&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 77 km and includes significant climbs, offering a full day's adventure.
tips: And after this, I recommend to go have a coffee and a brownie at Les Escales 🙂
Summit that is reached by a road between dense forests. Although there is a lot of shade, in summer it can be very humid and intensify the sensation of heat.
A nice little cafe in La Floresta. Here you can find coffee, sandwhiches, empanadas, salads, and soups. It has options for vegetarians and vegans.
